112897.explanation: Imbalance and quality matter for both men and women; preferably more women! The HB List always includes women on the candidate list (also high up) and has a candidate alderman.

112898.explanation: The municipal council is elected, so what is important? For the street/neighborhood or for everyone? Do not exclude a referendum (e.g., on a reorganization request).

112901.explanation: Not always, but if such a request comes in, assess if it is possible. For example, on the edges of villages and for suitable businesses. Also competition matters! We have favorable policies for campgrounds, but with gradations concerning size.

112908.explanation: Because of the climate, yes. Entrepreneurs who have it and use it do see benefits...

112912.explanation: We have already done this with subsidies; come in for associations and neighborhood buildings. Companies have been asked to pay attention to this! The HB List aims for inclusion and accessibility for everyone.

112917.explanation: It depends on the schools themselves, which sometimes already take this up (Music in the Classroom). Additionally through associations like fanfare/harmony bands, which we subsidize.

112921.explanation: It is an important task, but a community officer should ideally make himself unnecessary through good interventions with municipal policy (e.g., parking). Unfortunately, the reality is different!

112927.explanation: Only temporarily, for example during relocation or emergencies.

112928.explanation: Facilitate new construction within the urban area. Possibly allow transformation from community to residential use.

112929.explanation: As far as possible. For example, volunteer work at an organization or association is useful for social participation!

112930.explanation: What is a low income? Residents can apply and an assessment follows.

112931.explanation: What is the threshold and does it apply only to singles/families or...? Moreover, often assets are present. Apply tailored solutions for minimum-income groups and people who need help! According to the HB List, the municipality should think along preventively and proactively and assist.

112933.explanation: We already have many and the policy is that people can make one themselves (on their own property) plus sufficient in public spaces. Sometimes we remove them partially again if there is insufficient use.

112934.explanation: This is already arranged in Drimmelen by the HB List, except for truly main roads (and there are only a few).

112935.explanation: Care provision is important and must be available for everyone. But the CARE itself has priority over opinions etc. Moreover, capacity is limited.

112936.explanation: Help must be provided with the available capacity in terms of people and resources, but that help must also be effective and helpful!

112937.explanation: At an acceptable level according to inflation.

112939.explanation: Already quite high now. Differentiation is desirable.

112945.explanation: In consultation with schools and with sufficient swimming instructors! Align with the season; swimming and gym lessons are important. The HB List has already introduced wet gym class.

112946.explanation: We adopt the regional 30% social and 40% affordable. This must also include sufficient rental homes!

112947.explanation: In villages and logical locations, high-rise is possible, but maximum 4 floors fitting the environment (and available parking space!). Is it financially feasible?

112951.explanation: No signals of problems at this time.

112953.explanation: We already have one in the regional arrangements. Together we catch it.

112956.explanation: We have already introduced the Biesbosch flag and neighboring municipalities are following. Cost-benefit analysis including enforcement/control.

112897.explanation: People want a college that delivers. That requires the best aldermen, not ticking boxes. If each party chooses its strongest candidate, we get a decisive board that cares for housing, safety, and a municipality that works.

112898.explanation: People must be heard, that is paramount. Therefore, we choose municipal council members who take time for you, weigh all interests, and make decisions based on facts. Big choices require carefulness, not a simple yes or no. This way, we ensure decisions we can still support later.

112901.explanation: Our farmers are the heart of the countryside. To continue existing, they must be able to adapt. A farm shop or campsite can help with that. It gives farmers room to undertake and secure their company’s future.

112908.explanation: A heated terrace means life in the village. Terrace heating helps hospitality stay open longer, brings people together, and creates atmosphere. This keeps our villages lively, cozy, and pleasant to live in.

112909.explanation: Firework shows are not a municipal task. But associations and entrepreneurs with passion must be able to organize them safely. This way, New Year's remains a moment to celebrate together joyfully.

112912.explanation: With a disability, you just want to go to the bakery or realtor. Accessibility must not be a luxury. Adjusting can sometimes be costly for entrepreneurs, so together with the municipality, we look for smart and feasible solutions.

112917.explanation: Children should simply be able to do their hobbies, whether music, sports, or something else. Usually, parents pay for this themselves. If that is not possible, the municipality helps. This way, every child gets the chance to participate.

112921.explanation: We have already increased the number of community officers. Now they must mainly be visible on the streets and intervene where necessary: in case of repeated nuisance, speeding, and heavy traffic through our villages. This keeps the streets pleasant and safe.

112927.explanation: The housing shortage is large and every solution counts. But as long as national rules remain unclear, we do not want to take risks. As a small municipality, we prefer to solve the shortage with plans that certainly work and deliver affordable homes.

112928.explanation: Those who live here want to stay here. That only succeeds if we keep building. By developing new neighborhoods outside town centers, we enable affordable housing for young and old, with respect and in consultation with our farmers who own the land.

112929.explanation: Participation gives self-worth. Those who receive benefits and can, should give something back to society. This can also be voluntary. This keeps you active, gives you experience, and increases the chance of work or meaningful daily activities.

112930.explanation: Those with little to spend should not get stuck on municipal charges. Remission provides relief and prevents bigger problems. This way, we help people stay afloat and guide them towards work and a stable future.

112931.explanation: Those who need help must be able to turn to the municipality. But continuously raising the income threshold does not solve the problem. We choose targeted personal support and assistance towards work so that people truly move forward.

112933.explanation: The number of charging points has been significantly expanded; for now, there are enough. Many people do not yet drive electric and must be able to park their car on the street. This way, we keep space for everyone.

112934.explanation: Everyone wants to feel safe on the streets and get from A to B quickly. A fixed 30 km/h limit feels patronizing. That’s why we specifically target speeding offenders with mobile speed cameras in places residents experience as race tracks. This keeps things safe and sensible.

112935.explanation: Care should focus on quality, not on a label. Purchasing separate care for each religion makes it expensive and unnecessarily complicated. We choose good, affordable care that truly helps families.

112936.explanation: Young people need help that works and is nearby. Therefore, we look not only at the price but especially at what truly helps. At the same time, youth care must remain affordable so everyone receives the right support on time.

112937.explanation: Lowering the property tax (OZB) sounds nice, but it costs us the building of new houses, good roads, and safety. We do not want that. However, we ensure municipal charges don’t get out of hand so you have enough left at the end of the month.

112939.explanation: After years of stagnation, the tourist tax is now back in order. Visitors pay a fair amount so bicycle paths, sidewalks and our beautiful places are better maintained. This makes it nicer and safer for everyone.

112945.explanation: Every child must learn to swim. That’s important for their safety. But not everything important should be solved by schools. We believe this is primarily the parents’ responsibility.

112946.explanation: Young and old want to be able to keep living here. That requires balance. That’s why we build neighborhoods that suit our villages: with 30% social rental housing and space for owner-occupied homes. This keeps housing affordable for people with real ties to the area.

112947.explanation: Our villages must remain villages. That’s why we don’t build tall apartment blocks but a maximum of 3 to 5 floors. Only with a truly good plan can it be otherwise. This way, young people, families, and seniors can continue living here without having to move away.

112951.explanation: No one wants to park three streets away or be stuck in the street. Households increasingly have multiple cars. That’s why we choose additional parking spots in our villages so you can park your car normally and nearby.

112953.explanation: We do what is necessary to prevent fines, but extra shelter doesn’t fit here now. Our primary responsibility lies with starters, families, and seniors who cannot find housing. For them, we must first build affordable homes.

112955.explanation: You should feel safe in your own village, on the street, and in the evening. In places where that feeling is missing, camera surveillance helps tackle nuisance and crime. Targeted and sensible, so it becomes calm and safe again.

112956.explanation: We have just started with the Biesbosch badge. First, ensure all Biesbosch municipalities participate and that it works well. Only then will we consider further steps. No new boating tax, but first complete what we have jointly started.

112957.explanation: As a municipality, we must also think beyond four years. Everyone wants to travel easily: to work, school or family. Passenger transport over the railway line makes this easier. This way, our villages remain accessible, livable and attractive, now and for future generations.

112897.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes the municipal executive should reflect society well. An equal distribution of men and women leads to better decisions. Equal opportunities are not always self-evident, so they must be organized.

112898.explanation: Groen Drimmelen wants residents to have more influence on decisions. This can be done through participation and citizen assemblies. A referendum is not always the right tool, requires good conditions, and does not fit every topic.

112901.explanation: Groen Drimmelen is open to small-scale recreation and broadening activities, but not "always." Nature, livability, traffic and landscape must be taken into account in the decision.

112908.explanation: Using energy on terraces in this way is unnecessary waste. Groen Drimmelen wants to promote sustainable alternatives such as blankets and wind shields.

112909.explanation: Groen Drimmelen is critical of fireworks because of safety, animals and the environment. A central fireworks show can be an alternative, but only if there is support and the costs can be justified.

112912.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes everyone should be able to participate. With subsidies, loans and advice, you encourage accessible shops and hospitality for all and help entrepreneurs and residents by removing barriers.

112917.explanation: Culture and music aid talent development and wellbeing. Groen Drimmelen wants every child to have the opportunity to take music lessons. We remove financial barriers through subsidies.

112921.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes safety requires a good balance between prevention, a strong community and targeted enforcement. Extra enforcement is only used if other solutions are insufficient.

112927.explanation: Holiday homes are not a solution to housing shortages. This undermines recreational destinations and can lead to poor living quality and enforcement problems. Groen Drimmelen wants affordable, safe and legal homes. Housing shortages are solved with real homes.

112928.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes infill development is not always the best solution. It must not come at the expense of green space. Building outside the village boundary is possible, but only without damage to nature, biodiversity and the character and cultural heritage of villages.

112929.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes people on social assistance should not be obliged to do community work. Not everyone can do that. However, we encourage voluntary participation in activities.

112930.explanation: Remission helps residents with low incomes avoid debts and stress. Groen Drimmelen wants remission to be targeted and fair, granted automatically where possible, and part of broad poverty policy.

112931.explanation: More people with low or middle incomes struggle to make ends meet. Groen Drimmelen believes increasing the income threshold helps prevent working poor and families with high fixed costs and debts. Support must remain targeted, affordable and fair.

112933.explanation: Groen Drimmelen wants to encourage electric driving without harming livability. Charging points are mainly placed in smart locations like parking lots, centers, schools and businesses. Extra chargers in residential neighborhoods only come if really needed or with innovations that relieve the power grid.

112934.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes 30 km/h is good for the safety of children, elderly, pedestrians and cyclists. It reduces noise nuisance and emissions, making neighborhoods more livable and attractive.

112935.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes everyone should have access to good care. It is nice if care suits your faith or culture, but it must not come at the cost of quality, accessibility, or affordability.

112936.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes in youth care quality should be more important than price. Cheap care that doesn’t work well does not help children and families.

112937.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes property tax (OZB) must remain affordable for residents. Reduction is possible but must not cause important facilities like green spaces, culture and social work to diminish.

112939.explanation: Tourist tax can help with maintenance and facilities. Groen Drimmelen believes an increase is only responsible if it is fair and the municipality can truly do something with it.

112945.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes parents are primarily responsible for school swimming. We do want to help families who really cannot afford it so everyone can learn to swim safely.

112946.explanation: There are too few social rental homes in Drimmelen. Groen Drimmelen wants more affordable housing so everyone can find a place to live.

112947.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes tall residential flats do not fit in Drimmelen. Sometimes building a bit higher is possible if well suited to the environment and does not cause nuisance. Homes must remain human-scale and livable. We find apartment blocks too massive.

112951.explanation: Groen Drimmelen believes there should not be just more parking spaces, as that can reduce livability. We want smart parking arrangements so space remains for greenery, playing, and safe cycling and walking. This keeps our centers practical and pleasant for everyone.

112953.explanation: Groen Drimmelen wants to contribute to humane shelter for people fleeing war or violence. Extra shelter is possible but must be safe, small-scale, well organized and fitting the local environment.

112955.explanation: Groen Drimmelen values privacy and equal treatment. Camera surveillance is a serious measure and does not solve causes of insecurity. We use cameras cautiously and under strict conditions. The design and use of public space and people's behavior also play a role.

112956.explanation: Groen Drimmelen opposes a mandatory boating tax. We find it important that boating in the Biesbosch remains accessible to everyone, but nature and facilities must be well protected. That’s why we appreciate the voluntary Biesbosch badge.

112957.explanation: Groen Drimmelen considers good public transport important. However, a passenger service on this freight railway line is not realistic at the moment. The costs are high and it is uncertain how many people would use the train. We will continue to think along about smart and feasible public transport solutions for our residents.

112897.explanation: It is about quality, not gender, origin, and/or age.

112898.explanation: Residents should be able to express their opinion but not necessarily through a referendum. A referendum is very costly. Now, participation is also possible through discussion tables and participation policy.

112901.explanation: We believe a (farm) shop belongs to the farming business. We support "buy local." Good coordination is needed for a campsite.

112908.explanation: This is up to the entrepreneur themselves. Busy terraces contribute to a lively atmosphere in the villages.

112909.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. The fireworks ban is determined at the national level.

112912.explanation: We think it is important that buildings are accessible to all target groups. Entrepreneurs must take care of their own buildings.

112917.explanation: This is not a task for a municipality.

112921.explanation: No additional enforcement officers (BOA's). Our BOA's should have more authority and abilities, thus be granted broader powers. Enforcers instead of supervisors.

112927.explanation: No Fort Oranje in the municipality of Drimmelen.

112928.explanation: Agricultural land must remain agricultural. New construction must connect to the existing residential centers.

112929.explanation: Everyone contributes their part in this way.

112930.explanation: The municipality should focus more on guidance through, for example, a budget coach instead of debt forgiveness.

112931.explanation: This is not a municipal task but national policy.

112933.explanation: Charging points must be provided based on demand. Charging at home is preferred if possible.

112934.explanation: For clear roads, it is not necessary to maintain a 30 km/h zone. This can cause poor traffic flow and longer response times for emergency services.

112935.explanation: It is not always possible to accommodate this. We must especially be grateful to the people willing to provide this care.

112936.explanation: Quality of youth care takes priority over price.

112937.explanation: It must not be the cash cow of our municipality. Due to bidding up on houses, the sale price is no longer a realistic basis for determining property tax (OZB).

112939.explanation: We must not price ourselves out of the market as a municipality.

112945.explanation: This is a task for the parents.

112946.explanation: There is more demand for affordable homes to buy. In Drimmelen, a higher standard is already applied than is nationally required.

112947.explanation: We are not an urban area.

112951.explanation: Drimmelen meets the standards. Therefore, MORE parking spaces are not needed.

112953.explanation: If it were up to us, no extra asylum reception centers would be established.

112955.explanation: Where necessary, we want more camera surveillance to increase the sense of security.

112956.explanation: The Biesbosch is not solely on Drimmelen's territory. Our municipality cannot and may not make decisions about it.

112957.explanation: The railway is not designed for that, so this is not realistic. ProRail decides.

112898.explanation: We are not in favor of a binding referendum.

112901.explanation: We want to think along with the farmers so they can continue to earn a good living.

112908.explanation: In the spirit of energy saving, we say: It should be banned. But it attracts more customers if they can stay outside longer. And then we support the entrepreneur, so no ban.

112909.explanation: It is a nice moment to jointly ring in the new year and still enjoy fireworks.

112912.explanation: The entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for this.

112917.explanation: Music is an added value for a child's development.

112921.explanation: Rules you agree on must be able to be checked.

112927.explanation: This causes distortion in the housing market.

112928.explanation: Infill locations have priority. Then build outside the village boundaries.

112929.explanation: We want to actively think along with them to be socially involved.

112930.explanation: These people should be helped with debt counseling.

112931.explanation: People who get into trouble should be helped. This varies per situation.

112933.explanation: This takes parking spaces away from other residents.

112935.explanation: It is about the right care. This is separate from faith.

112939.explanation: Has just increased. It must remain attractive to recreate here.

112945.explanation: Parents are responsible for this themselves.

112946.explanation: This is a good ambition given the high demand for it.

112947.explanation: This should be possible on the edges of the villages.

112951.explanation: It must not be at the expense of green space in the neighborhood.

112953.explanation: We do want to comply with the national obligations.

112955.explanation: Only if it is really necessary. Privacy!

112956.explanation: The Biesbosch voucher has been introduced for this purpose.

112957.explanation: There are enough alternatives conceivable. All railway crossings should be adapted.

112897.explanation: The pursuit of equality is good. The Drimmelen municipal executive normally consists of only 4 people. This makes achieving this ambition difficult.

112898.explanation: Referendums often reinforce divisions in society. Through participation, residents should be involved and heard.

112901.explanation: We have many fine examples of this in our municipality.

112908.explanation: An attractive terrace during cold periods improves the atmosphere in our villages, we should not block this beforehand.

112909.explanation: After the national ban, we should organize a nice common alternative together in every center. This way, we can try to prevent nuisance from private fireworks.

112912.explanation: It is up to entrepreneurs themselves to comply with accessibility requirements.

112917.explanation: Just as with sports clubs, subsidies should also apply to music associations.

112921.explanation: We have already become stronger, but adherence to our joint rules can always use more support.

112927.explanation: This is not the right solution for the housing problem. It will mainly promote housing for labor migrants and not help permanent home seekers.

112928.explanation: Especially in places like Hooge Zwaluwe Zuid and Kalkvliet in Terheijden, we can build more houses on the edges of villages.

112929.explanation: Contributing to the local community is very important, for example as a volunteer at clubs.

112930.explanation: By helping people with a low income with debt forgiveness, we ensure they do not fall further behind.

112931.explanation: The limit does not need to be raised, but those entitled now should be better helped. Currently, too few people use this aid, even though they are entitled to it.

112933.explanation: We must not put further pressure on parking issues. We can place more charging stations, but these spots should not automatically be designated for electric driving only.

112934.explanation: 50 km/h where it is safe, 30 km/h where it must be! For traffic flow, it is a bad idea to drive everywhere at 30 km/h.

112935.explanation: Of course, only when a choice is available.

112936.explanation: Price can never be the decisive factor when it comes to the future of our youth.

112937.explanation: This is not feasible if we want to achieve all the objectives for our villages.

112939.explanation: Especially through the dedicated fund from the tourist tax and the voucher for the Biesbosch, we can work on new facilities for an even stronger tourist climate.

112945.explanation: It goes too far to burden education with this task. Obtaining a swimming diploma should be paid for people with less money. We do support initiatives like 'wet gym class'.

112946.explanation: The target is now for 30% of new homes to be social housing. We have not reached this yet and must first grow towards it.

112947.explanation: We should build higher up to four or five stories, but not higher than that.

112951.explanation: Parking spaces are a large part of public space. Currently, there is no acute parking problem in the centers, so there is no need for more now.

112953.explanation: Ensuring safety is our priority. We currently accommodate 75 people in Made. To meet our task under the dispersion law, we must also accommodate 75 people elsewhere in our municipality. We are against larger locations and only support small-scale shelter.

112955.explanation: At unsafe places, this is a good solution!

112956.explanation: It is precisely with the revenue from this tax that we can improve jetties and other facilities in the Biesbosch.

112957.explanation: This is not a priority for the CDA and costs our municipality a lot of time and money.

112897.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen stands for equal rights and opportunities for everyone but ultimately prioritizes quality.

112898.explanation: New initiatives for more citizen participation, such as a referendum or a citizen panel, contribute to better participation and we welcome them. The municipal council remains the decision-making authority.

112901.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen believes that each case should be considered individually to determine if it is possible.

112908.explanation: We consider banning terrace heaters a form of unnecessary nannying. Of course, we do encourage our residents to be mindful of their energy consumption.

112909.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen is cautious about fireworks. They are harmful to air quality and cause nuisance and health risks for people and animals. Therefore, we are considering alternatives, such as a centralized fireworks or light show.

112912.explanation: Entrepreneurs are responsible for the layout of their own buildings. They must comply with laws and regulations regarding accessibility.

112917.explanation: For children who need it but whose parents cannot afford it, this can be applied for through the Leergeld foundation. The municipality should promote group music lessons at primary schools.

112921.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is not necessarily against more enforcement officers (BOAs). With the current number of five BOAs, we meet the standard for a municipality like Drimmelen. So this should be sufficient.

112927.explanation: If the rules that allow permanent residence in holiday homes are met, this is permitted as far as GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen is concerned.

112928.explanation: In certain locations outside the built-up area, in the peripheral core area, GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen supports building, but only on sites designated in the environmental vision.

112929.explanation: If someone wants to do volunteer work, that is obviously fine, but we will not force anyone.

112930.explanation: The gap between rich and poor is growing in the Netherlands, including in the municipality of Drimmelen. If we don’t assist these people, for example by waiving debts, they will get into even more trouble with all the resulting consequences.

112931.explanation: The gap between rich and poor is growing in the Netherlands, including in the municipality of Drimmelen. If we don’t assist these people, for example by waiving debts, they will get into even more trouble with all the resulting consequences.

112933.explanation: We ensure sufficient charging points, aligned with the growth in the number of electric cars. This keeps electric driving accessible and practical for everyone-without overcapacity or backlog. The expansion is smart and village-focused, so charging remains possible in smaller towns as well.

112934.explanation: We want to improve traffic safety in our villages. Where necessary, the speed limit will be reduced to 30 km/h.

112935.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen believes we cannot impose this. We consider it a free choice.

112936.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen believes that purchasing mainly high-quality care is what we should prioritize. If it is cheaper price-wise, that is a bonus.

112937.explanation: The Property Tax (OZB) is a source of income for the municipality that is indexed annually. Lower OZB means less money to spend. If other income can compensate this in the long term, the OZB may be lowered if possible.

112939.explanation: As far as GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen is concerned, the tourist tax, like other taxes, should be indexed annually according to the established inflation rate.

112945.explanation: Swimming is healthy and important for young people, so we encourage children and youth to move and swim. School swimming can contribute to this, so we allocate funds for it.

112946.explanation: Regional agreements have been made with the regional municipality, setting the social housing percentage at 30%. To keep housing projects somewhat financially viable, we believe it is wise to adhere to the agreed percentages from the housing deal.

112947.explanation: Many residents are looking for a home, and GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen believes more houses can be built by building upwards-not everywhere and not too high. Where possible, this should fit the village character and the environment.

112951.explanation: There are enough parking spaces in our municipality. In all spatial planning, we apply the STOMP principle-Walking, Cycling, Public Transport, Mobility as a Service (meaning shared cars), and Private Cars.

112953.explanation: Only if this is required based on (national or regional) agreements, for example with the introduction of the spatial spread act.

112955.explanation: Only if there is a reason, such as safety or excessive nuisance. These cameras are temporarily deployed as long as needed.

112956.explanation: We are currently trialing the Biesbosch flag, from which we receive income from each visitor arriving by their own boat in the Biesbosch. We will evaluate this and may make the flag mandatory based on that. This makes the introduction of a boating tax unnecessary.

112957.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Drimmelen wants to primarily focus on good bus connections. We are not necessarily opposed to it, but the current railway line is unsuitable and ends at the Weststad industrial area. Profitable and well-functioning passenger transport on this route is expensive and thus unrealistic.

112897.explanation: It would be good if the college were equally divided between men and women. Our starting point is the quality of aldermen, not gender.

112898.explanation: For topics with a major impact on the living environment, we support a referendum.

112901.explanation: Yes, this is already possible based on the environmental plan and must definitely remain so.

112908.explanation: We see no problem with terrace heating. It adds some buzz and coziness to nightlife and provides extra income for our hospitality entrepreneurs.

112909.explanation: We are not in favor of this. Aside from the costs and the use of administrative capacity, it will be difficult to find a suitable location within the municipality that is attractive to all residents of all towns.

112912.explanation: Entrepreneurs are primarily responsible for making their buildings accessible for people with disabilities. The municipality can provide support in this.

112917.explanation: In Drimmelen, it is possible to take music lessons at our music associations, and subsidies per child are provided for this.

112921.explanation: During this council period, the capacity of enforcement officers (BOAs) has been increased from two to five. This has already considerably expanded capacity, making further expansion not immediately necessary.

112927.explanation: We have relatively few holiday homes in Drimmelen. We want to keep these available for tourists.

112928.explanation: To meet the demand caused by the housing shortage, it is necessary to allow new construction at the boundaries of our towns.

112929.explanation: We find it acceptable that people on social assistance benefits perform socially useful work. This also gives them a better chance of finding regular employment.

112930.explanation: Residents with low incomes already struggle enough financially. Waiving municipal taxes helps with this, but only under strict conditions to prevent abuse.

112931.explanation: People who need help must be able to access it. There are national standards for this.

112933.explanation: There are increasingly more electric cars, so it is necessary to add more charging points. The right locations for these must be carefully considered and discussed with the local community.

112934.explanation: We support a 30 km/h speed limit within built-up areas, except for main roads.

112935.explanation: In our opinion, this is undesirable and unnecessarily increases costs.

112936.explanation: We must certainly keep an eye on costs to control the expenses of youth care. Youth care should always be accessible to young people. This involves a good balance between price and quality.

112937.explanation: To maintain our facilities, it is necessary to levy property tax. This tax should not rise more than the inflation adjustment to keep the burden on our residents manageable.

112939.explanation: Provided that this is also spent on recreational facilities.

112945.explanation: Learning to swim is important and should be accessible to all children. This also leads to better use of our swimming pools.

112946.explanation: Building enough rental homes is absolutely necessary to meet the needs of those seeking housing. It is essential that enough is built for all target groups and that there are also sufficient affordable owner-occupied homes.

112947.explanation: Drimmelen is a rural municipality where tall residential towers do not belong. However, it should be possible to build apartments of up to three or four floors in new housing areas.

112951.explanation: Wherever possible, additional parking spaces should be created to prevent parking nuisance.

112953.explanation: Drimmelen already has an asylum seekers’ center. We are absolutely against a second asylum seekers’ center in Drimmelen and will not agree to it.

112955.explanation: This is to prevent vandalism and crime and to increase the feeling of safety among our residents.

112956.explanation: We already have the Biesbosch flag, which is in fact a tax.

112957.explanation: This is unrealistic as it would require the entire infrastructure to be adapted.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in the villages.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Drimmelen should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Residents of Drimmelen should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Drimmelen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce a boating tax for everyone who wants to sail in the Biesbosch with their own boat.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
High-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Drimmelen.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should push for passenger transport on the freight rail line between Lage Zwaluwe and Oosterhout.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: At least 40% of newly built homes in Drimmelen should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Drimmelen moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: An additional reception facility for asylum seekers should be provided.

titleParts.text: Er moet een extra opvang voor

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.
